texto: Now, think of a square, a living, beautiful square. And imagine that it must tell you about itself, about its life. You understand, a square would scarcely ever think of telling you that all its four angles are equal: this has become so natural, so ordinary to it that it's simply no longer consciously aware of it. And so with me: I find myself continually in this square's position.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Zamyatin Yevgeny sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Now, think of a square, a living, beautiful square. And imagine that it must tell you about itsel…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, identity, normal, útil em momentos de vida e normal.
